response.justification: The candidate has a long and varied engineering career with genuine strengths in distributed systems and software craftsmanship, but critically fails the primary minimum qualification: 8 years of C++ experience. C++ appears only in a brief role at National Instruments (roughly 6 months, 2000–2001) and a bachelor thesis project, and is absent from the listed skills section entirely. The role centers on leading a C++ cloud compute platform, yet the candidate's career is overwhelmingly built on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web and product engineering. Cloud infrastructure depth is also shallow — limited to AWS CDK in a pricing microservice and Kubernetes for a personal open-source project — well short of the production-scale platform ownership this role demands.
strengths: High-scale distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O design
Consistent software craftsmanship culture across roles: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and active knowledge-sharing through workshops, dojos, and public talks
Long career breadth spanning embedded, mobile, web, and infrastructure work demonstrates technical adaptability and full-stack range
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — approximately 6 months at National Instruments in 2000–2001 and a bachelor thesis; C++ does not appear in the listed skills, making the 8-year minimum unmet by a very wide margin
No evidence of technical leadership over a cloud compute or platform engineering organization; leadership examples are mentoring-level or project-scoped, not cross-team platform strategy and delivery

Cloud architecture exposure is shallow — AWS CDK for a direct-mail pricing service and self-managed Kubernetes for a personal open-source project fall far short of the production cloud platform ownership the role requires
